news.title:
Experts recommend 6 stocks for the short term, including Axis Bank, Nykaa, Coal India, Wipro, Trent, and CCL Products.
Key targets: Axis Bank (₹1,343-1,365), Nykaa (₹275-282), Coal India (₹500), Wipro (₹220), Trent (₹4,100), CCL (₹1,210-1,240).

news.title:
Coal India (CIL) plans to auction 25.62 MT coal in April to address energy supply issues amid West Asia crisis.
CIL to expand SWMA auctions to Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal from Jan 2026, enhancing transparency and market integration.
news.summary:
Coal India (CIL) plans to auction 25.62 MT coal in April to address energy supply issues amid West Asia crisis.
CIL to expand SWMA auctions to Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal from Jan 2026, enhancing transparency and market integration.

news.title:
MCL and BCGCL sign land lease for a 2,000 TPD coal-to-ammonium nitrate project.
The project at Lakhanpur, Jharsuguda, marks progress in India's coal gasification efforts.

news.title:
Coal India reported a 1.5% decline in March coal production to 84.5 MT YoY.
Coal offtake rose 0.7% YoY in March, reaching 69.5 MT from 69 MT.
news.summary:
Coal India reported a 1.5% decline in March coal production to 84.5 MT YoY.
Coal offtake rose 0.7% YoY in March, reaching 69.5 MT from 69 MT.

news.title:
Coal India's March 2026 coal offtake rose 0.7% YoY to 69.5 MT.
This increase indicates mild demand growth during the month.
news.summary:

news.title:
Coal India Ltd's (CIL) provisional production for Mar'26 was 84.5 MT, down 1.5% YoY.
CIL's FY Apr'25-Mar'26 production totaled 768.1 MT, a decline of 1.7% YoY.

news.title:
Analysts recommend Nalco, ONGC, Coal India, MRPL, and GAIL for buy, citing technical and sectoral trends.

news.title:
MarketSmith India recommends Strides Pharma and Coal India as buys, highlighting strong fundamentals and technical breakout setups.
Nifty 50 fell 2.1% to 22,331.40; Sensex dropped 2.2% amid risk-off sentiment from West Asia war and surging crude prices.

news.title:
Coal India's sales rose in March after six months amid gas shortfall, summer demand.
The sales uptick aligns with coal stock ramp-up for peak summer amid U.S.-Iran tensions.

news.title:
Coal India aims to boost production to 1B tonnes by FY28-29, despite Q3FY26 revenue falling 5.2% YoY to ₹34,924 crore.
Geopolitical tensions and higher power demand may drive e-auction prices; stock upgraded to 'Buy' with ₹506 target.
news.summary:
